name: Array
summary: The SketchUp Array class adds additional methods to the standard Ruby Array class. Specifically, it contains methods allowing an array to behave just as a Geom::Vector3d or Geom::Point3d object (which can be thought of as arrays of 3 coordinate values). Therefore, you can use the Array class in place of a Geom::Point3d or Geom::Vector3d as a way to pass coordinate values.

methods.name: cross
methods.summary: The #cross method is used to compute the cross product between two vectors.
methods.remarks: The #cross method is used to compute the cross product between two vectors.

methods.name: distance
methods.summary: The #distance method is used to compute the distance between two points.
methods.remarks: The #distance method is used to compute the distance between two points.

methods.name: distance_to_line
methods.signature: distance_to_line(point, vector) => Length | distance_to_line(point1, point2) => Length
methods.summary: The #distance_to_line method is used to compute the distance from a Geom::Point3d object to a line.
methods.remarks: The #distance_to_line method is used to compute the distance from a Geom::Point3d object to a line.

methods.name: distance_to_plane
methods.signature: distance_to_plane(point, vector) => Length | distance_to_plane(point1, point2, point3) => Length | distance_to_plane(float1, float2, float3, float4) => Length | distance_to_plane(array) => Length | distance_to_plane(array) => Length | distance_to_plane(array) => Length
methods.summary: The #distance_to_plane method is used to compute the distance from a Geom::Point3d object to a plane.
methods.remarks: The #distance_to_plane method is used to compute the distance from a Geom::Point3d object to a plane.

returns.doc: The distance between the two points.

methods.name: dot
methods.summary: The #dot method is used to compute the dot product between two vectors.
methods.remarks: The #dot method is used to compute the dot product between two vectors.

methods.name: normalize
methods.signature: normalize => Array(Float, Float, Float) | normalize => Array(Float, Float)
methods.summary: Note: The arguments and return value will be converted to a floating point value.
methods.remarks: Note: The arguments and return value will be converted to a floating point value. (Unlike in the Geom::Vector3d#normalize! method.) The #normalize method is used to normalize a vector (setting its length to 1). It returns a new array rather than changing the original in place.

returns.doc: an array object representing a vector

methods.name: normalize!
methods.signature: normalize! => Array(Float, Float, Float) | normalize! => Array(Float, Float)
methods.summary: The #normalize! method is used to normalize a vector in place (setting its length to 1).
methods.remarks: The #normalize! method is used to normalize a vector in place (setting its length to 1).

returns.doc: an array object representing a vector

methods.name: offset
methods.signature: offset(vector) => Array(Length, Length, Length) | offset(vector) => Array(Length, Length) | offset(vector, length) => Array(Length, Length, Length) | offset(vector, length) => Array(Length, Length)
methods.summary: The #offset method is used to offset a point by a vector.
methods.remarks: The #offset method is used to offset a point by a vector. it returns a new array rather than modifying the original in place.

params.name: vector
params.doc: A Vector3d object used to offset the point.

returns.doc: The newly offset array representing a point or vector.

methods.name: offset!
methods.signature: offset!(vector) => Array(Length, Length, Length) | offset!(vector) => Array(Length, Length) | offset!(vector, length) => Array(Length, Length, Length) | offset!(vector, length) => Array(Length, Length)
methods.summary: The #offset! method is used to offset a point by a vector.
methods.remarks: The #offset! method is used to offset a point by a vector. The array is modified in place.

params.name: vector
params.doc: A Vector3d object used to offset the point.

returns.doc: The newly offset array representing a point or vector.

methods.name: on_line?
methods.signature: on_line?(point, vector) => Boolean | on_line?(point1, point2) => Boolean
methods.summary: The #on_line? method is used to determine if a Geom::Point3d object is on a line.
methods.remarks: The #on_line? method is used to determine if a Geom::Point3d object is on a line.

returns.doc: true if the point is on the line, false if the point is not on the line.

methods.name: on_plane?
methods.signature: on_plane?(point, vector) => Boolean | on_plane?(point1, point2, point3) => Boolean | on_plane?(float1, float2, float3, float4) => Boolean | on_plane?(array) => Boolean | on_plane?(array) => Boolean | on_plane?(array) => Boolean
methods.summary: The #on_plane? method is used to determine if a Geom::Point3d object is on a plane (to within SketchUp's standard floating point tolerance).
methods.remarks: The #on_plane? method is used to determine if a Geom::Point3d object is on a plane (to within SketchUp's standard floating point tolerance).

methods.name: project_to_line
methods.signature: project_to_line(point, vector) => Array(Length, Length, Length) | project_to_line(point1, point2) => Array(Length, Length, Length)
methods.summary: The #project_to_line method is used to retrieve the projection of a Geom::Point3d object onto a line.
methods.remarks: The #project_to_line method is used to retrieve the projection of a Geom::Point3d object onto a line.

returns.doc: A new point on the line that is closest to this point

methods.name: project_to_plane
methods.signature: project_to_plane(point, vector) => Array(Length, Length, Length) | project_to_plane(point1, point2, point3) => Array(Length, Length, Length) | project_to_plane(float1, float2, float3, float4) => Array(Length, Length, Length) | project_to_plane(array) => Array(Length, Length, Length) | project_to_plane(array) => Array(Length, Length, Length) | project_to_plane(array) => Array(Length, Length, Length)
methods.summary: The #project_to_plane method retrieves the projection of a Geom::Point3d onto a plane.
methods.remarks: The #project_to_plane method retrieves the projection of a Geom::Point3d onto a plane.

methods.name: transform
methods.signature: transform(transform) => Array<Length, Length> | transform(transform) => Array<Length, Length, Length>
methods.summary: The #transform method is used to apply a Geom::Transformation or Geom::Transformation2d object to a Geom::Point3d or Geom::Point2d object defined by an Array object.
methods.remarks: The #transform method is used to apply a Geom::Transformation or Geom::Transformation2d object to a Geom::Point3d or Geom::Point2d object defined by an Array object. This method returns a new Array object instead of modifying the original.

methods.name: transform!
methods.summary: Note: This method modifies the original.
methods.remarks: Note: This method modifies the original. The #transform! method is used to apply a Geom::Transformation object to a Geom::Point3d object defined by an Array object.

methods.name: vector_to
methods.summary: The #vector_to method is used to create an array as a vector from one point to a second point.
methods.remarks: The #vector_to method is used to create an array as a vector from one point to a second point.

properties.name: x
properties.summary: The #x method retrieves the x coordinate.
properties.remarks: The #x method retrieves the x coordinate.

properties.name: y
properties.summary: The #y method retrieves the y coordinate.
properties.remarks: The #y method retrieves the y coordinate.

properties.name: z
properties.summary: The #z method retrieves the z coordinate.
properties.remarks: The #z method retrieves the z coordinate.
